tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and mild ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy, despite a low HbA1c. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment for non-diabetic causes of renal dysfunction are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
